works.title: The Concept of Nature (Early-mid (preceding Science and the Modern World, 1925))
works.authorDate: Alfred North Whitehead · 1920 (the Tarner Lectures, Trinity College Cambridge)
works.form: Lectures in eight chapters · Process philosophy / philosophy of nature
works.tagline: Against the "bifurcation of nature" into primary and secondary qualities — Whitehead's 1920 philosophy of nature as a single integrated event-process

works.title: Three Rival Versions of Moral Enquiry (Late (third volume of the After Virtue trilogy))
works.authorDate: Alasdair MacIntyre · 1990 (the Gifford Lectures, University of Edinburgh, 1988)
works.form: Lecture series in ten chapters · Contemporary virtue ethics / tradition-constituted enquiry
works.tagline: Three rival modes of contemporary moral enquiry — Encyclopaedic, Genealogical, Traditioned — with the case for the Thomist-Aristotelian Traditioned version

works.title: Letter from Birmingham Jail (Mid (the canonical theological-political document))
works.authorDate: Martin Luther King Jr. · April 16, 1963 (written in jail in response to a published statement by eight Alabama clergymen criticising King's direct-action methods)
works.form: Open letter / theological-political essay · American Civil Rights / black-church theology / non-violent direct-action
works.tagline: "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere" — King's 7,000-word jail-cell letter defending non-violent direct action and the Christian-prophetic foundations of civil rights

works.title: Strength to Love (Mid (the major collection of sermons))
works.authorDate: Martin Luther King Jr. · 1963 (collected sermons; some preached at Dexter Avenue Baptist Church Montgomery in the 1950s)
works.form: Collection of seventeen sermons · American Civil Rights / black-church preaching
works.tagline: King's major book of theological-pastoral preaching — Christian love as the principle of non-violent social transformation

works.title: Gravity and Grace (Posthumous (Weil died in 1943 at age 34))
works.authorDate: Simone Weil · 1947 (posthumous; assembled from Weil's notebooks by Gustave Thibon)
works.form: Posthumous anthology of aphorisms drawn from notebooks · Twentieth-century French mystical philosophy / Christian-Platonist
works.tagline: Gravity (pesanteur) and grace — Simone Weil's posthumous mystical-philosophical aphorisms on attention, affliction, and the void

works.title: The Need for Roots (Posthumous)
works.authorDate: Simone Weil · 1943 (written for Free France in London in the months before Weil's death; published posthumously 1949)
works.form: Political-philosophical treatise in three parts · Twentieth-century French political philosophy / Christian-Platonist
works.tagline: A declaration of duties before rights — Weil's posthumous political-philosophical prelude for post-war reconstruction, focused on the deep human need for rootedness

works.title: Waiting for God (Posthumous)
works.authorDate: Simone Weil · 1942 letters to Father Perrin; published posthumously 1950
works.form: Posthumous collection of letters and essays · Twentieth-century French mystical theology / Christian-Platonist
works.tagline: Letters and essays to Father Perrin — Weil's most extended account of her religious experience, her relation to the Church, and her refusal of baptism

works.title: Sanctorum Communio (Earliest (Bonhoeffer's dissertation at age 21))
works.authorDate: Dietrich Bonhoeffer · 1927 (Bonhoeffer's doctoral dissertation, completed at age 21)
works.form: Doctoral dissertation in theology · German Lutheran-evangelical theology / theological sociology
works.tagline: The communion of the saints as a theological-sociological reality — Bonhoeffer's 21-year-old dissertation that Karl Barth called "a theological miracle"

works.title: The Seven Storey Mountain (Early (Merton's breakthrough book; the spiritual autobiography of his conversion))
works.form: Spiritual autobiography in three parts · American Catholic / Trappist monastic spirituality
works.tagline: A modern Augustine's Confessions — Merton's 1948 narrative of his conversion to Catholicism and entry into the Trappist monastery at Gethsemani

works.title: Seeds of Contemplation (1949) / New Seeds of Contemplation (Mid-late (Merton's mature contemplative theology))
works.authorDate: Thomas Merton · 1961 (expanded revision of Seeds of Contemplation, 1949)
works.form: Theological-contemplative essays in thirty-nine chapters · American Catholic / Trappist contemplative spirituality
works.tagline: The mature statement of Merton's contemplative spirituality — the false self and the true self, contemplative prayer as participation in the divine life

works.title: Love and Responsibility (Early (his major pre-papal work; drawn from pastoral and academic teaching))
works.authorDate: Karol Józef Wojtyła / Pope John Paul II · 1960 (the first major theological-philosophical book of the future John Paul II; based on his pastoral and academic teaching)
works.form: Philosophical-pastoral treatise in five chapters · Polish personalism / Catholic moral theology / Lublin School
works.tagline: The personalist norm and the ethics of sexual love — Wojtyła's 1960 treatise developing the philosophical foundation of his later theology of the body

works.title: The Acting Person (Mid (his major academic-philosophical work, before his 1978 papal election))
works.authorDate: Karol Józef Wojtyła / Pope John Paul II · 1969 (the philosophical magnum opus of his pre-papal academic career)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in seven chapters · Polish phenomenological personalism / Lublin School
works.tagline: Person and act — Wojtyła's phenomenological-Thomistic analysis of human action as the constitution and self-revelation of the person

works.title: Hind Swaraj (Early (the founding text of Gandhi's mature political-philosophical vision))
works.authorDate: Mohandas K. Gandhi · 1909 (written aboard the Kildonan Castle in ten days during the voyage from London to South Africa)
works.form: Dialogue between Editor and Reader, in twenty chapters · Indian political philosophy / anti-colonial thought
works.tagline: Indian home rule as the rejection of British civilisation itself — Gandhi's 1909 dialogue establishing the philosophical foundation of his subsequent satyagraha

works.title: An Autobiography: The Story of My Experiments with Truth (Late-mid (looking back over the formative years))
works.authorDate: Mohandas K. Gandhi · 1925-29 (originally serialised in the weekly Navajivan; the chapters cover Gandhi's life through the early Indian campaigns up to 1921)
works.form: Spiritual-political autobiography in five parts · Indian political philosophy / spiritual autobiography
works.tagline: The "experiments with truth" — Gandhi's spiritual-political journey from childhood through early satyagraha, the major source for his self-understanding

works.title: Mishneh Torah (Mid (the major legal work, between the early Commentary on the Mishnah and the late Guide of the Perplexed))
works.authorDate: Moses Maimonides (Rambam) · c. 1170-80 (the second of Maimonides's three major works; preceding the Guide of the Perplexed of c. 1190)
works.form: Comprehensive legal code in fourteen books · Medieval Jewish theology and law
works.tagline: The "Second Torah" — Maimonides's comprehensive code organising the entire Jewish legal tradition into fourteen systematic books

works.title: Sister Outsider (Mid (the major prose collection of Lorde's career))
works.authorDate: Audre Lorde · 1984 (collecting essays and speeches from the 1970s and early 1980s)
works.form: Collection of fifteen essays and speeches · Black feminist thought / intersectional theory
works.tagline: "The master's tools will never dismantle the master's house" — Lorde's 1984 collection of essays on race, gender, sexuality, age, and class as inseparable dimensions of identity and struggle

works.title: Tales of the Hasidim (Late (Buber's mature engagement with the Hasidic tradition))
works.authorDate: Martin Buber · 1947 (The Early Masters); 1948 (The Later Masters); compiled over decades of Buber's engagement with Hasidism
works.form: Two-volume anthology of Hasidic stories with introductions · Twentieth-century Jewish renewal / Hasidic spirituality
works.tagline: The major Western anthology of Hasidic stories — Buber's lifelong work of recovering Hasidic spirituality for modern Jewish and broader religious life

works.title: A Black Theology of Liberation (Early (the systematic founding text of the field))
works.authorDate: James Cone · 1970 (the second of Cone's books and the systematic statement of the position announced in Black Theology and Black Power, 1969)
works.form: Systematic theological treatise · Black liberation theology
works.tagline: "God is black" — Cone's 1970 systematic theology, the founding text of black liberation theology

works.title: The Cross and the Lynching Tree (Late (Cone's major late book))
works.form: Theological-historical book in six chapters · Black liberation theology
works.tagline: The cross of Christ and the American lynching tree as parallel sites of innocent suffering — Cone's late major work integrating theological symbolism with the historical record of racial violence

works.title: Gitanjali (Mid (the Nobel-winning collection))
works.authorDate: Rabindranath Tagore · 1910 (Bengali original); 1912 (Tagore's own English prose translation)
works.form: Collection of 103 devotional poems / prose-poems · Bengali Hindu devotional / Brahmo Samaj philosophical poetry
works.tagline: "Where the mind is without fear" — Tagore's Nobel-winning 1910 collection of devotional poems addressed to the divine Beloved, the central source of his international fame

works.title: Sadhana: The Realisation of Life (Mid (the major philosophical prose statement))
works.authorDate: Rabindranath Tagore · 1913 (the Hibbert Lectures, Harvard; published 1913)
works.form: Lectures in eight chapters · Bengali Hindu philosophical-religious thought / Brahmo Samaj
works.tagline: Sadhana — the realisation of life. Tagore's 1913 Harvard lectures presenting Vedantic-philosophical religious thought for Western audiences

works.title: No Future Without Forgiveness (Late (the major reflective work after the TRC))
works.authorDate: Desmond Tutu · 1999 (the personal-theological reflection on the work of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission, 1995-98)
works.form: Theological-political memoir · South African ubuntu theology / Anglican liberation theology
works.tagline: Ubuntu and forgiveness — Archbishop Tutu's 1999 personal-theological account of the South African Truth and Reconciliation Commission

works.title: Nausea (Early (Sartre's first novel, before Being and Nothingness))
works.form: Novel in diary form · French existentialism / phenomenological-literary
works.tagline: Antoine Roquentin's diary in Bouville — the contingency of existence revealed through the famous chestnut-tree scene, the literary embodiment of early Sartrean existentialism

works.title: No Exit (Mid (alongside Being and Nothingness))
works.authorDate: Jean-Paul Sartre · 1944 (first performed at the Théâtre du Vieux-Colombier in May 1944)
works.form: One-act play with four characters · French existentialist theatre
works.tagline: "Hell is other people" — Sartre's most famous play, three characters in a hotel-room hell, the dramatic embodiment of his analysis of being-for-others

works.title: Critique of Dialectical Reason (Late (Sartre's major late philosophical work))
works.authorDate: Jean-Paul Sartre · 1960 (vol. I); vol. II unfinished, published posthumously 1985
works.form: Multi-volume systematic philosophical treatise · French existentialist-Marxism
works.tagline: Existentialism integrated with Marxism — Sartre's major late philosophical attempt to ground historical-materialist analysis in existentialist phenomenology

works.title: Crime and Punishment (Mid (the first of Dostoevsky's great late novels))
works.authorDate: Fyodor Mikhailovich Dostoevsky · 1866 (serialised in The Russian Messenger)
works.form: Novel in six parts and an epilogue · Russian realist novel / philosophical-psychological fiction
works.tagline: Raskolnikov's axe-murder and his slow psychological-spiritual reckoning — Dostoevsky's 1866 novel that opens the great late period

works.title: Notes from Underground (Mid (the transition into the great late period))
works.form: Short novel in two parts · Russian existentialist-philosophical fiction
works.tagline: "I am a sick man... I am a spiteful man" — Dostoevsky's 1864 narrative of the Underground Man, often called the first existentialist novel

works.title: The Idiot (Mid (after Crime and Punishment, before Demons and Karamazov))
works.authorDate: Fyodor Mikhailovich Dostoevsky · 1868-69 (serialised in The Russian Messenger)
works.form: Novel in four parts · Russian realist novel / philosophical-religious fiction
works.tagline: Prince Myshkin, the "positively good man" — Dostoevsky's attempt to portray Christian goodness in fallen Russian society

works.title: Demons (Mid-late (the third of the four great novels))
works.authorDate: Fyodor Mikhailovich Dostoevsky · 1871-72 (based partly on the 1869 Nechayev affair)
works.form: Novel in three parts · Russian political-philosophical fiction
works.tagline: Stavrogin, Verkhovensky, Kirillov — Dostoevsky's 1872 prophetic critique of nineteenth-century Russian revolutionary nihilism

works.title: The Fall (Late (Camus's last completed novel; the Nobel followed in 1957))
works.form: Short novel as extended monologue · French existentialist literature
works.tagline: Jean-Baptiste Clamence's confession-monologue in an Amsterdam bar — Camus's 1956 dissection of bourgeois moral self-deception

works.title: Moral Man and Immoral Society (Early-mid (Niebuhr's breakthrough book that established Christian realism))
works.form: Theological-political treatise in ten chapters · American Christian realism / Protestant social ethics
works.tagline: Individual moral capacity vs collective moral failure — Niebuhr's 1932 book that established Christian realism against liberal Protestant optimism

works.title: The Irony of American History (Late (Niebuhr's major Cold War political-theological book))
works.form: Theological-political essay in eight chapters · American Christian realism / political theology
works.tagline: The "ironic" structure of American national self-understanding — Niebuhr's 1952 Christian-realist analysis of Cold War American politics

works.title: Lectures on the Philosophy of History (Late (Berlin lectures of the 1820s, his mature mature))
works.authorDate: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel · 1822-31 (delivered as lectures); 1837 (compiled and published posthumously by Eduard Gans)
works.form: Posthumous lecture series compiled from notes · German idealism / philosophy of history
works.tagline: World history as the progress of the consciousness of freedom — Hegel's posthumous lectures organising the historical-cultural process under the dialectical-idealist framework

works.title: Lectures on Aesthetics (Late (Berlin lectures))
works.authorDate: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel · 1820s (delivered as lectures); 1835-38 (compiled and published posthumously by H. G. Hotho)
works.form: Posthumous lectures in three volumes · German idealist philosophy of art
works.tagline: Art as the sensible manifestation of the Idea — Hegel's posthumous three-volume systematic philosophy of art

works.title: Encyclopaedia of the Philosophical Sciences (Mature (the most comprehensive single-text statement of the system))
works.authorDate: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel · 1817 (1st edition); 1827 (2nd edition); 1830 (3rd and definitive edition, in three volumes)
works.form: Systematic philosophical encyclopaedia in three volumes · German absolute idealism
works.tagline: Hegel's entire philosophical system in one organised text — Logic, Philosophy of Nature, Philosophy of Spirit

works.title: Madness and Civilization (Early (Foucault's breakthrough work, his doctoral dissertation))
works.form: Historical-philosophical study · French postmodernism / Foucauldian archaeology
works.tagline: The history of madness in the classical age — Foucault's 1961 doctoral dissertation that opened his career-long project

works.title: The Birth of the Clinic (Early-mid (between Madness and Civilization and The Order of Things))
works.form: Historical-philosophical study in ten chapters · French postmodernism / Foucauldian archaeology
works.tagline: The emergence of modern medical perception — Foucault's 1963 archaeology of the "medical gaze" and the modern clinic

works.title: The Archaeology of Knowledge (Mid (methodological transition between archaeological and genealogical phases))
works.form: Methodological-philosophical treatise · French postmodernism / philosophy of historical method
works.tagline: The methodological reflection on Foucault's archaeological method — discursive formations, statements, the "positive unconscious" of knowledge

works.title: Scivias (Early (the first of her three major visionary works))
works.authorDate: Hildegard of Bingen · 1141-51 (composed in the decade after Hildegard's call to write, ten years after entering the monastic life)
works.form: Visionary theological treatise in three books, with twenty-six visions · Medieval German Christian mysticism / Rhineland visionary tradition
works.tagline: Twenty-six visions of the divine — Hildegard's 1141-51 founding work of medieval German visionary theology

works.title: Liber Divinorum Operum (Late (the culmination of her visionary trilogy))
works.authorDate: Hildegard of Bingen · 1163-73 (composed in the last decade of Hildegard's life, after the Scivias and the Liber Vitae Meritorum)
works.form: Visionary cosmological-theological treatise in three parts, with ten visions · Medieval German Christian mysticism / cosmological-theological synthesis
works.tagline: The "Cosmic Man" — Hildegard's late cosmological-theological synthesis integrating creation, anthropology, and divine providence

works.title: On Christian Doctrine (Mid-late (composed across three decades))
works.authorDate: Augustine of Hippo · 397 (Books 1-3.25); 426-27 (Books 3.25-4, completed near the end of Augustine's life)
works.form: Theological-rhetorical treatise in four books · Latin patristic theology / Christian biblical interpretation
works.tagline: The reading and preaching of Scripture — Augustine's comprehensive treatise on Christian biblical interpretation and rhetorical communication

works.title: Principles of Political Economy (Mid (Mill's major economic work))
works.authorDate: John Stuart Mill · 1848 (1st edition); revised through 1871 (7th edition)
works.form: Systematic economic-philosophical treatise in five books · Classical political economy / utilitarianism
works.tagline: The major nineteenth-century English political economy — Mill's 1848 synthesis of classical economic theory with progressive social-political philosophy

works.title: Philosophical Fragments (Mid (the same productive 1844 as Concept of Anxiety))
works.authorDate: Søren Kierkegaard · 1844 (published under the pseudonym Johannes Climacus)
works.form: Short pseudonymous philosophical-theological treatise · Danish religious existentialism
works.tagline: Can a historical event be the starting point for eternal consciousness? — Kierkegaard's 1844 fragments developing the question that Concluding Unscientific Postscript will pursue at length

works.title: Practice in Christianity (Late (the last major pseudonymous work; preceding the attack on the Danish state church))
works.authorDate: Søren Kierkegaard · 1850 (published under the pseudonym Anti-Climacus)
works.form: Pseudonymous theological treatise in three parts · Danish religious existentialism
works.tagline: The practice of Christian discipleship — Kierkegaard's 1850 most demanding existential-theological work, the proximate prelude to his attack on the Danish state church

works.title: Repetition (Early-mid (the same explosive 1843 as Either/Or and Fear and Trembling))
works.authorDate: Søren Kierkegaard · 1843 (published the same day as Fear and Trembling, under the pseudonym Constantin Constantius)
works.form: Short pseudonymous philosophical-literary work · Danish religious existentialism
works.tagline: Repetition as the modern category replacing Greek recollection — Kierkegaard's 1843 experimental short work on the structures of memory, freedom, and Christian temporality

works.title: A System of Logic (Early (Mill's first major book, the foundation of his philosophical reputation))
works.authorDate: John Stuart Mill · 1843 (Mill's first major book); revised through 1872 (8th edition)
works.form: Systematic treatise on logic in six books · British empiricism / philosophy of science
works.tagline: The major nineteenth-century English logic — Mill's 1843 systematic treatise including the famous Methods of experimental inquiry

works.title: Time and the Other (Early (the breakthrough early work, before Totality and Infinity))
works.authorDate: Emmanuel Levinas · 1946-47 (delivered as four lectures at Collège philosophique); published 1948
works.form: Four lectures · French phenomenology / Jewish religious philosophy
works.tagline: Time, the Other, the asymmetry of the ethical relation — Levinas's 1948 lectures developing the foundational categories of his mature phenomenology

works.title: Existence and Existents (Early (the first major book, before Time and the Other))
works.authorDate: Emmanuel Levinas · 1935-46 (largely composed in a German prisoner-of-war camp); published 1947
works.form: Phenomenological-philosophical treatise · French phenomenology / Jewish religious philosophy
works.tagline: The phenomenology of existence as such — Levinas's 1947 first major book, developing the categories of fatigue, indolence, insomnia, and the il y a

works.title: Difficult Freedom (Mid (alongside Totality and Infinity))
works.form: Collection of essays on Judaism · Twentieth-century Jewish religious philosophy
works.tagline: Essays on Judaism — Levinas's 1963 collection that develops the explicitly Jewish-religious dimension of his philosophical-ethical project

works.title: Liber Vitae Meritorum (Mid (the middle volume of the visionary trilogy))
works.authorDate: Hildegard of Bingen · 1158-63 (the middle work of the visionary trilogy, between Scivias and Liber Divinorum Operum)
works.form: Visionary moral-spiritual treatise in six parts, with thirty-five vices and thirty-five virtues · Medieval German Christian mysticism / spiritual psychology
works.tagline: Thirty-five vices and thirty-five virtues — Hildegard's middle visionary work, the systematic spiritual-moral treatise

works.title: The Imaginary (Early (preceding Being and Nothingness))
works.form: Phenomenological-psychological treatise · French phenomenology / philosophy of mind
works.tagline: The phenomenological psychology of imagination — Sartre's 1940 analysis of imaginative consciousness as the unique mode of consciousness

works.title: Lectures on the Philosophy of Religion (Late (Berlin lectures))
works.authorDate: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel · 1821-31 (delivered as lectures); 1832 (compiled and published posthumously)
works.form: Posthumous lecture series in three parts · German idealism / philosophy of religion
works.tagline: World religions arranged in the dialectical-developmental framework — Hegel's posthumous lectures on the philosophy of religion, culminating in "the consummate religion" of Christianity

works.title: De Brevitate Vitae (Mid)
works.form: Short philosophical essay / dialogue · Roman Stoicism
works.tagline: "It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it" — Seneca's short Stoic meditation on the proper use of time

works.title: De Providentia (Late)
works.authorDate: Lucius Annaeus Seneca · c. 64 AD (late in Seneca's life, shortly before his forced suicide)
works.form: Short philosophical essay / dialogue · Roman Stoicism

works.title: Prior and Posterior Analytics
works.authorDate: Aristotle · c. 350 BC (the core logical works of the Organon)
works.form: Two-part systematic treatise on logic · Classical Greek philosophy / Aristotelian logic
works.tagline: The systematic theory of syllogism (Prior) and demonstrative science (Posterior) — Aristotle's major logical works at the heart of the Organon

works.title: On the Heavens
works.form: Cosmological treatise in four books · Classical Greek philosophy / Aristotelian cosmology
works.tagline: The geocentric cosmos — Aristotle's major cosmological treatise, the foundation of pre-Copernican Western cosmology

works.title: On Nature (Fragments)
works.authorDate: Heraclitus of Ephesus · c. 500 BC (the fragments preserved through later authors' quotations)
works.form: Collected fragments of an originally lost work · Pre-Socratic Greek philosophy

works.title: Parisian Questions (Mid-late)
works.authorDate: Meister Eckhart (Eckhart von Hochheim) · c. 1300-1326 (the scholastic-Latin works composed across Eckhart's academic career)
works.form: Scholastic disputations and treatises · Medieval German Christian mysticism / Dominican scholasticism
works.tagline: Eckhart's scholastic Latin works — the philosophical-systematic framework underlying his vernacular German sermons

works.title: Discourse on Metaphysics (Mid (Leibniz's breakthrough philosophical statement))
works.authorDate: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz · 1686 (sent to Antoine Arnauld; not published in Leibniz's lifetime)

works.title: Praise of Folly (Mid (Erasmus's most widely read book))
works.authorDate: Desiderius Erasmus of Rotterdam · 1509 (composed during a visit to Thomas More); 1511 (first published)

works.title: Adagia (Long (composed across Erasmus's entire mature career))

works.tagline: The classical-proverbial wisdom of the ancient world, collected and commented — Erasmus's 1500-1536 Adagia, the major Renaissance source for ancient wisdom

works.form: Systematic psychological treatise · Depth psychology / analytical psychology

works.title: Modern Man in Search of a Soul (Mid-late (mature systematic statement))
works.authorDate: Carl Gustav Jung · 1933 (essay collection, English translation by Cary F. Baynes)
works.form: Collection of eleven essays · Depth psychology / analytical psychology
works.tagline: The mature accessible introduction to Jungian analytical psychology — depth psychology applied to the modern spiritual condition

works.form: Systematic psychological treatise in seven chapters · Depth psychology / psychoanalysis
works.tagline: "The interpretation of dreams is the royal road to a knowledge of the unconscious activities of the mind" — Freud's 1900 founding work of psychoanalysis

works.title: Mrs Dalloway (Mid (the first major modernist novel of Woolf's maturity))
works.form: Modernist novel · British modernism / stream-of-consciousness fiction

works.tagline: The Window, Time Passes, The Lighthouse — Woolf's 1927 novel structured by the death and absence at its tripartite centre

works.title: Hamlet (Mid (mature middle period))
works.form: Tragedy in five acts · English Renaissance drama
works.tagline: "To be or not to be" — Shakespeare's c. 1600 tragedy, the longest and most-cited of his plays, the central work of English Renaissance drama

works.title: King Lear (Mid-late (the major tragedies))

works.tagline: Lear's descent into madness on the heath — Shakespeare's greatest tragedy, the most extreme exploration of suffering, ingratitude, and the bare human

works.title: Confessions of a Mask (Early (the breakthrough novel that established Mishima's literary reputation))
works.authorDate: Yukio Mishima · 1949 (Mishima's breakthrough novel, written at age 24)

works.title: Kitáb-i-Íqán (Mid (pre-declaration in 1863))
works.authorDate: Bahá'u'lláh (Mírzá Ḥusayn-ʻAlí Núrí) · 1862 (composed in Baghdad in two days and two nights, in response to questions from one of the Báb's maternal uncles)

works.title: Kitáb-i-Aqdas (Late (the major late doctrinal-legal book))
works.authorDate: Bahá'u'lláh (Mírzá Ḥusayn-ʻAlí Núrí) · 1873 (in 'Akká, the prison-city where Bahá'u'lláh was exiled)

works.title: Reason, Truth and History (Mid (the major mid-career book, the systematic statement of internal realism))
