works.tagline: In dark times, the moral lucidity of particular individuals lights what general theory cannot illuminate

works.title: Wittgenstein on Rules and Private Language (Mature (Kripke's second major book after Naming and Necessity, 1980))
works.authorDate: Saul Kripke · 1982 (Harvard UP; based on 1976 Wolfson College lecture, 1977 Princeton seminars)
works.form: Philosophical monograph · Twentieth-century analytic philosophy of language / Wittgenstein scholarship
works.tagline: There is no fact of the matter about what a rule "requires" — the meaning skeptic's challenge is the real heart of Wittgenstein's later philosophy

works.title: On What Matters (Late (Parfit's final, three-decade-in-the-making work — his second after Reasons and Persons, 1984))
works.authorDate: Derek Parfit · 2011 (Vols I & II, Oxford UP); 2017 (Vol III, Oxford UP — published months after Parfit's death)
works.form: Three-volume philosophical treatise · Twenty-first-century analytic ethics / moral realism
works.tagline: Kantian ethics, contractualism, and rule-consequentialism climb the same mountain from different sides — and what we ought to do is independently real

works.title: An Historical and Moral View of the French Revolution (Late (Wollstonecraft's last completed major non-fiction work, three years before her death))
works.authorDate: Mary Wollstonecraft · 1794 (Vol. I only — the projected continuation was never written)
works.form: Historical-philosophical narrative · British radical Enlightenment / republicanism
works.tagline: The principles of 1789 are sound — the Terror is their corruption, not their fulfilment

works.title: Thoughts on the Education of Daughters (Early (Wollstonecraft's first published book, written from her experience as a governess and a school proprietress))
works.form: Conduct manual / educational essay · British dissenting Enlightenment / women's educational reform
works.tagline: A rational education makes a rational woman — and the first task of the educator is to free the female mind from the constant pressure to please

works.title: Aion (Late (one of Jung's last and most ambitious works, written in his mid-seventies))
works.authorDate: Carl Gustav Jung · 1951 (Aion: Untersuchungen zur Symbolgeschichte, Rascher, Zurich; English trans. R.F.C. Hull, Collected Works vol. 9, pt II, 1959)
works.form: Depth-psychological / religious-symbolic treatise · Analytical psychology / Jungian depth psychology
works.tagline: The Christ-symbol is the symbol of the Self — and the historical "age of Aion" demands the integration of the shadow that the Christ-image has projected outward

works.title: The Red Book (Middle (the personal experimental record from which all of Jung's later theoretical work emerged))
works.authorDate: Carl Gustav Jung · 1914-30 (composed in calligraphic script with painted illuminations; published 2009 by W. W. Norton, ed. Sonu Shamdasani)
works.form: Illuminated manuscript / depth-psychological visionary text · Analytical psychology / visionary literature
works.tagline: The seed-text of Jungian depth psychology — Jung's personal record of the active-imagination experiments that produced the archetypal framework

works.title: The Undiscovered Self (Late (one of Jung's last short works, written at 82))
works.authorDate: Carl Gustav Jung · 1957 (Schweizer Monatshefte; book edition Rascher, Zurich; English trans. R.F.C. Hull, Atlantic Monthly Press, 1958)
works.form: Cultural-psychological essay · Analytical psychology / Cold War cultural criticism
works.tagline: The mass society and the totalitarian state are the external symptoms of an inner condition — the undiscovered Self

works.title: De Officiis (Late (Cicero's last completed philosophical work, written in the months before his proscription and execution))
works.authorDate: Marcus Tullius Cicero · 44 BC (composed at Tusculum, October-December 44 BC, in the months between Caesar's assassination and Cicero's own death in December 43 BC)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in three books, in the form of a letter to his son Marcus · Roman Stoicism / Latin philosophical literature
works.tagline: Where the honourable and the useful seem to conflict, the conflict is illusory — the honourable is always also the truly useful

works.title: De Re Publica (Mid-mature (Cicero's political philosophical synthesis, composed during the breakdown of the late Republic))
works.authorDate: Marcus Tullius Cicero · 54-51 BC (composed during a period of political withdrawal from active life)
works.form: Philosophical dialogue in six books (partly fragmentary; Books I-II and the closing Somnium Scipionis substantially preserved) · Roman political philosophy / republicanism
works.tagline: The best constitution is mixed — combining the strengths of monarchy, aristocracy, and democracy — and the just statesman serves a cosmic order that outlasts his political career

works.title: Tusculan Disputations (Late (composed in the year of Cicero's daughter's death, in his most intense period of philosophical writing))
works.authorDate: Marcus Tullius Cicero · 45 BC (Tusculanae Disputationes; composed at Tusculum after the death of his daughter Tullia)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in five books, in dialogue form · Roman Stoicism / Latin philosophical consolation literature
works.tagline: Virtue alone suffices for the happy life — death is not an evil, pain is bearable, the passions can be governed

works.title: Convention: A Philosophical Study (Early (Lewis's first book, published at 28, the year he began at UCLA))
works.authorDate: David Lewis · 1969 (Harvard UP; based on his 1967 Harvard PhD dissertation under W. V. O. Quine)
works.form: Philosophical monograph in five chapters · Analytic philosophy of language / formal pragmatics
works.tagline: A convention is a stable solution to a coordination problem — and the conventions of language are not arbitrary but rationally selected for mutual benefit

works.title: Miracles: A Preliminary Study (Mature (after Mere Christianity and Screwtape; the most philosophical of Lewis's apologetic works))
works.authorDate: C. S. Lewis · 1947 (Bles, London; revised 1960 chapter 3 after Anscombe's 1948 Socratic Club critique)
works.form: Philosophical apologetics · Twentieth-century Anglican apologetics / Christian philosophy
works.tagline: Naturalism is self-refuting — and if naturalism fails, the antecedent improbability of miracles disappears

works.title: Disputed Questions on Truth (Early-mature (Aquinas's first major work after the Sentences commentary))
works.authorDate: Thomas Aquinas · 1256-59 (Paris, during Aquinas's first regency)
works.form: Disputed questions (29 questions, 253 articles) · High medieval scholasticism / Thomistic theology
works.tagline: Truth is the conformity of intellect with thing — the foundational scholastic treatment of truth, knowledge, and divine cognition

works.title: On Evil (Late (Aquinas's mature treatment of evil and the passions, parallel to the Summa))
works.authorDate: Thomas Aquinas · 1269-72 (Paris, during Aquinas's second regency, contemporaneous with Summa Theologiae I-II)
works.form: Disputed questions (16 questions, 101 articles) · High medieval scholasticism / Thomistic moral metaphysics
works.tagline: Evil is the privation of a due good — not a positive reality, and the ground of every defect from physical pain to original sin

works.title: Compendium of Theology (Late (begun during the Roman regency, unfinished at Aquinas's death))
works.authorDate: Thomas Aquinas · 1265-67 (begun in Rome, broken off after Aquinas's 1273 mystical experience)
works.form: Theological compendium (organised around faith, hope, and charity) · High medieval scholasticism / Thomistic theology
works.tagline: A compact synthesis of the whole of theology around the three theological virtues — Aquinas's short summary for his secretary

works.title: Colloquia (Mature (the work that grew through Erasmus's most productive decades and was repeatedly enlarged))
works.authorDate: Desiderius Erasmus of Rotterdam · 1518 (first edition Familiarium Colloquiorum Formulae); enlarged 1519, 1522, 1524, 1526, 1529, 1533
works.form: Dialogues · Northern Renaissance humanism / Latin Christian satire
works.tagline: Latin conversation exercises that became a vehicle for satirical-philosophical Christianity — and a perennial Protestant-Catholic battlefield

works.title: De Libero Arbitrio (Late (the treatise that publicly broke the Erasmus-Luther alliance, written after seven years of pressure for Erasmus to declare his position))
works.authorDate: Desiderius Erasmus of Rotterdam · 1524 (De Libero Arbitrio ΔΙΑΤΡΙΒΗ sive Collatio, Froben, Basel)
works.form: Theological treatise · Northern Renaissance humanism / Catholic moderate reform
works.tagline: The Bible itself does not univocally settle the question of free will — therefore we should hold the moderate position the Fathers and the Church have always held

works.title: Greek New Testament (Novum Instrumentum) (Mature (the work that established Erasmus's international reputation and reshaped biblical scholarship))
works.authorDate: Desiderius Erasmus of Rotterdam · 1516 (Novum Instrumentum omne, Froben, Basel — first edition); revised 1519, 1522, 1527, 1535
works.form: Critical edition with translation and annotations · Northern Renaissance philology / Christian humanism
works.tagline: Return to the sources — the Greek New Testament made available, with new Latin translation, against the inherited Vulgate

works.title: On Learned Ignorance (Mature (the founding work of Cusa's philosophical career, composed at age 39))
works.authorDate: Nicholas of Cusa (Nicolaus Cusanus) · 1440 (composed on the return voyage from the failed Council of Florence union with the Greeks)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in three books · Late medieval / Renaissance Christian Platonism / Neoplatonic mystical theology
works.tagline: The infinite Maximum cannot be known by finite reason — and recognising this is itself the highest knowledge

works.title: The Vision of God (Mature (one of Cusa's most condensed and beautiful late works))
works.authorDate: Nicholas of Cusa (Nicolaus Cusanus) · 1453 (composed for the Benedictine monks of Tegernsee, sent with an icon of an all-seeing face)
works.form: Mystical theological meditation · Late medieval / Renaissance Christian Platonism / Neoplatonic mystical theology
works.tagline: The all-seeing icon meets every viewer's gaze — and so does the God whose seeing is the source of all things' being

works.title: On Conjectures (Mature (the systematic epistemological development of the docta-ignorantia framework))
works.authorDate: Nicholas of Cusa (Nicolaus Cusanus) · c. 1442-43 (composed shortly after De Docta Ignorantia, dedicated to Cardinal Cesarini)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in two books · Late medieval / Renaissance Christian Platonism / epistemology
works.tagline: All finite knowledge is conjecture — approximation to a truth never fully attained — and recognising this is itself the condition of progress

works.title: Anti-Pelagian writings (Late (Augustine's last great theological controversy, occupying the final two decades of his life))
works.authorDate: Augustine of Hippo (Aurelius Augustinus) · 412-30 (the long anti-Pelagian controversy); peak works 426-29
works.form: Controversial theological treatises · Patristic Latin theology / North African Catholic Christianity
works.tagline: Grace alone — without prevenient grace the human will, after the Fall, can do nothing salvific

works.title: Opus Tripartitum (Late (Eckhart's most ambitious Latin project, undertaken in the years before the 1326 trial))
works.authorDate: Meister Eckhart (Eckhart von Hochheim) · c. 1311-26 (planned during Eckhart's second Paris regency, never completed; only fragments survive)
works.form: Unfinished systematic theological-philosophical project · Late medieval Latin scholasticism / Rhineland mysticism
works.tagline: A unified Latin scholastic system organising 1,000 propositions on being, the soul, and God — never completed; only fragments and the biblical commentaries survive

works.title: Reden der Unterweisung (Early (Eckhart's first major vernacular work, written before the trials of his last decade))
works.authorDate: Meister Eckhart (Eckhart von Hochheim) · c. 1294-98 (Eckhart's early period as Prior of Erfurt and Vicar of Thuringia, before the first Paris regency)
works.form: Spiritual conferences in 23 chapters · Early Rhineland mysticism / German Dominican spiritual instruction
works.tagline: Detachment, "innerness," and the abandonment of self-will — the foundations of the contemplative life

works.title: Vom Edlen Menschen (Mature (probably from the Strasbourg years before the trial))
works.authorDate: Meister Eckhart (Eckhart von Hochheim) · c. 1308-13 (Strasbourg or Paris period)
works.form: Spiritual treatise · Rhineland mysticism / German Dominican spiritual writing
works.tagline: The noble man is the soul that has known itself in its divine ground — and that ground is one with God's own ground

works.title: The Dialogue of Divine Providence (Late (composed in Catherine's last two years, in the midst of her efforts to reform the Church and end the Avignon papacy))
works.authorDate: Catherine of Siena (Caterina Benincasa) · c. 1377-78 (composed by dictation in ecstatic states; Catherine could read with difficulty and probably could not write)
works.form: Mystical dialogue between the soul and God · Late medieval Italian mysticism / Dominican lay piety
works.tagline: The truth of the Mystical Body, the bridge of Christ, and the reform of the Church — Catherine's ecstatic-political mysticism dictated to scribes

works.title: Physica and Causae et Curae (Mid-mature (Hildegard's middle period, between her three major visionary works))
works.authorDate: Hildegard of Bingen · c. 1150-58 (Rupertsberg, between Scivias and Liber Vitae Meritorum)
works.form: Natural-philosophical and medical treatises · Twelfth-century monastic natural philosophy / medieval medicine
works.tagline: The plants, animals, stones, and elements have powers given by God for human healing — a complete medieval natural-medical encyclopedia

works.title: Against Marcion (Mature (Tertullian's longest and most systematic work))
works.authorDate: Quintus Septimius Florens Tertullianus · c. 207-12 (composed in three revisions; the third recension is the surviving text)
works.form: Theological polemic in five books · Early Latin Christian theology / North African Christianity
works.tagline: There is one God — the Creator of the Old Testament and the Father of Jesus Christ — and the unity of the two Testaments witnesses to it

works.title: Against Praxeas (Late (composed in Tertullian's Montanist period but with orthodox Trinitarian content))
works.authorDate: Quintus Septimius Florens Tertullianus · c. 213 (in Tertullian's Montanist period)
works.form: Theological treatise · Early Latin Christian theology / North African Trinitarianism
works.tagline: There are three Persons in one Substance — the formula "tres personae, una substantia" enters Christian theology

works.title: On the Resurrection of the Flesh (Mature (one of Tertullian's longest and most carefully argued treatises))
works.form: Theological polemic · Early Latin Christian theology / North African Christianity
works.tagline: The same flesh that lived and died will rise — the bodily resurrection is the whole point of the Christian hope, and to spiritualise it is to abandon Christianity

works.title: Kyōgyōshinshō (Mature)
works.form: Six-fascicle doctrinal compilation · Jōdo Shinshū (True Pure Land Buddhism)
works.tagline: Salvation by Other Power (tariki) — Amida's vow grants birth in the Pure Land to those who entrust themselves to it

works.title: Tannishō (Posthumous (the principal popular source for Shinran's teaching))
works.authorDate: Shinran · c. 1290 (compiled by Yuien-bō about 30 years after Shinran's death)
works.form: Short compilation of remembered sayings with corrective comments · Jōdo Shinshū
works.tagline: Even the good person is saved; how much more the evil — Shinran's most-quoted teachings, preserved against corruption

works.title: Ocean of Reasoning (Mature (Tsongkhapa's major philosophical-Madhyamaka work))
works.form: Verse-by-verse commentary on Nāgārjuna's Mūlamadhyamakakārikā · Tibetan Vajrayāna Buddhism / Gelug Madhyamaka
works.tagline: A verse-by-verse defense of Prāsaṅgika Madhyamaka — Nāgārjuna through Candrakīrti, against alternative Tibetan readings

works.title: Al-Hikmat al-Muta'aliya fi'l-Asfar al-'Aqliyya al-Arba'a (Late (the synthesis of his entire mature philosophy))
works.authorDate: Mulla Sadra (Sadr al-Din al-Shirazi) · composed over Mulla Sadra's mature life, completed c. 1638
works.form: Nine-volume philosophical encyclopedia in four journeys (asfar) · Islamic philosophy / Shi'a falsafa / School of Isfahan
works.tagline: The primacy of existence over essence — and the doctrine of substantial motion, change at the level of substance, not just accident

works.title: al-Kashf ʿan Manāhij al-Adilla (Mature)
works.form: Theological treatise · Islamic philosophy / Andalusian falsafa
works.tagline: The Ash'arite kalām is defective; the proper proof of God is the philosophical-Aristotelian one that the Qur'ān itself endorses

works.title: Seven Valleys and Four Valleys (Early (composed before the 1863 proclamation))
works.authorDate: Bahá'u'lláh (Mírzá Ḥusayn-ʻAlí Núrí) · c. 1856 (Seven Valleys) and c. 1858 (Four Valleys), both Baghdad period
works.form: Sufi mystical-poetic letters · Bahá'í Faith / Sufi mystical tradition
works.tagline: The mystical seeker passes through seven valleys to attain the presence of the Beloved

works.title: Letters to the Son of the Wolf (Last (less than a year before his 1892 death))
works.form: Polemical-pastoral epistle · Bahá'í Faith
works.tagline: A defense of the Bahá'í Faith and a survey of its essential teachings — addressed to an Iranian cleric who had ordered Bahá'í executions

works.title: Jōdo Wasan (Late)
works.form: Devotional hymns in Japanese verse · Jōdo Shinshū
works.tagline: Devotional poetry of the Pure Land — Amida's vow, the Pure Land, the lineage of masters who transmitted the Way

works.title: My Bondage and My Freedom (Mature (Douglass's second autobiography, written after his break with Garrison and the founding of his own newspaper))
works.authorDate: Frederick Douglass · 1855 (Miller, Orton & Mulligan, New York)
works.form: Autobiography · Nineteenth-century American abolitionist literature
works.tagline: A more philosophical autobiography than the 1845 Narrative — Douglass after the Garrisonian break, reflecting on natural rights, the meaning of citizenship, and the universal aspiration to freedom

works.title: Life and Times of Frederick Douglass (Late (Douglass's third autobiography, covering his post-1855 political career))
works.authorDate: Frederick Douglass · 1881 (Park Publishing, Hartford); expanded edition 1892 (De Wolfe, Fiske, Boston)
works.form: Autobiography · Nineteenth-century American political memoir
works.tagline: The Civil War, Reconstruction, and post-Reconstruction America — Douglass's political-philosophical reckoning with the unfinished work of emancipation

works.title: Sidereus Nuncius (Early-mid (the breakthrough that established Galileo's international reputation))
works.form: Astronomical pamphlet · Early modern natural philosophy / observational astronomy
works.tagline: The telescope has shown what no eye had seen: mountains on the moon, satellites of Jupiter, the resolution of the Milky Way into uncountable stars

works.title: The Assayer (Mature (composed during the brief honeymoon between Galileo and the new Pope Urban VIII))
works.form: Polemical natural-philosophical treatise · Early modern natural philosophy
works.tagline: The book of nature is written in the language of mathematics — without which one wanders in vain through a dark labyrinth

works.title: Letter to the Grand Duchess Christina (Mature (composed at the height of the developing controversy with Rome))
works.authorDate: Galileo Galilei · 1615 (composed; circulated in manuscript; first published 1636 in Strasbourg)
works.form: Long theological-methodological letter · Early modern natural philosophy / Catholic biblical hermeneutics
works.tagline: Scripture teaches how to go to heaven, not how the heavens go — natural philosophy must be free to follow its own evidence

works.title: Custer Died for Your Sins (Mature (Deloria's breakthrough book, written at 36))
works.form: Polemical political-philosophical essays · Twentieth-century Native American political philosophy / Lakota intellectual tradition
works.tagline: Native Americans are not vanishing — they are nations within a nation, with distinct legal, philosophical, and spiritual standing that demands recognition

works.title: The Metaphysics of Modern Existence (Mature (Deloria's most ambitious philosophical work))
works.form: Philosophical-metaphysical treatise · Twentieth-century Native American philosophy / Lakota intellectual tradition
works.tagline: The Native metaphysics of relation, place, and event offers a corrective to Western philosophy's preoccupation with substance, time, and the isolated subject

works.title: Race Matters (Mature (the book that established West as a major public intellectual))
works.form: Public-intellectual essay collection · African American prophetic-Christian tradition / late twentieth-century cultural criticism
works.tagline: Race matters in America — and the failure to address race as a structural condition rather than a personal attitude has kept American democracy from its promise

works.title: The American Evasion of Philosophy (Mature (West's major work of intellectual history, written before the Race Matters celebrity))
works.form: Intellectual history / philosophical genealogy · Late twentieth-century American philosophy / pragmatist tradition
works.tagline: American pragmatism is the distinctive philosophical tradition that grew up around the American intellectual evasion of European epistemology — and it has prophetic resources for the present

works.title: Democracy Matters (Late-mature (the post-9/11 sequel to the 1993 Race Matters))
works.form: Public-intellectual essay collection · African American prophetic-Christian tradition / contemporary cultural criticism
works.tagline: American democracy faces three dogmas — free-market fundamentalism, aggressive militarism, and escalating authoritarianism — and democratic renewal requires confronting each

works.title: Dictionnaire philosophique (Late (composed during the Ferney years))
works.authorDate: Voltaire (François-Marie Arouet) · 1764 (Dictionnaire philosophique portatif, Geneva; greatly expanded through 1769)
works.form: Philosophical dictionary · French Enlightenment
works.tagline: A portable Enlightenment — short, witty, polemical entries assaulting religious fanaticism and defending tolerance, reason, and natural rights

works.title: Traité sur la tolérance (Late (the campaign-treatise of the Ferney period))
works.authorDate: Voltaire (François-Marie Arouet) · 1763 (Traité sur la tolérance à l'occasion de la mort de Jean Calas)
works.form: Polemical-philosophical treatise · French Enlightenment
works.tagline: Religious tolerance is not a courtesy to be granted by the majority but the natural right of every human being — the Calas case shows what its denial costs

works.title: Éléments de la philosophie de Newton (Mid (the work that established Voltaire as a public intellectual of European reach))
works.authorDate: Voltaire (François-Marie Arouet) · 1738 (Éléments de la philosophie de Newton, Amsterdam; revised 1741)
works.form: Popular-scientific philosophical treatise · French Enlightenment / Newtonian natural philosophy
works.tagline: Newton's natural philosophy — gravitation, optics, the calculus, the empirical method — explained for the educated French-reading public, against the entrenched Cartesianism of the Sorbonne

works.title: Political Treatise (Late (Spinoza's last work, left incomplete at his death))
works.authorDate: Baruch (Benedict) Spinoza · 1675-77 (unfinished at Spinoza's 1677 death; published posthumously as part of the Opera Posthuma)
works.form: Unfinished political-philosophical treatise · Early modern political philosophy
works.tagline: The application of the Ethics's natural-rights framework to political institutions — and the most sustained early-modern defense of democratic constitutional government

works.title: Treatise on the Emendation of the Intellect (Early (Spinoza's first major philosophical project, left incomplete as the Ethics took shape))
works.authorDate: Baruch (Benedict) Spinoza · c. 1661-62 (unfinished; published posthumously in the Opera Posthuma 1677)
works.form: Methodological-philosophical treatise (unfinished) · Early modern epistemology and ethics
works.tagline: The proper method of knowing — and the proper end of human life — both arise from the careful emendation of the intellect away from the distracting goods of fortune toward the love of an unchanging good

works.title: Short Treatise on God (Early (Spinoza's first systematic presentation of his metaphysics, predating the Ethics))
works.authorDate: Baruch (Benedict) Spinoza · c. 1660-62 (Dutch manuscript circulated only among Spinoza's closest correspondents during his lifetime; rediscovered 1862)
works.form: Philosophical-theological treatise in two parts · Early modern philosophy
works.tagline: The early draft of the Ethics — Spinoza's first systematic presentation of his metaphysics, written before the more-Latin geometric form was fully developed

works.title: Principles of Cartesian Philosophy (Early (Spinoza's first published work))
works.authorDate: Baruch (Benedict) Spinoza · 1663 (Renati Des Cartes Principiorum Philosophiae Pars I et II, Amsterdam: Rieuwertsz)
works.form: Geometrical-deductive philosophical treatise · Early modern philosophy / Cartesian rationalism
works.tagline: A geometric reconstruction of Descartes — Spinoza's only work published under his own name during his lifetime, and the methodological forerunner of the Ethics

works.title: Discourse on the Sciences and Arts (Early (the work that launched Rousseau's career))
works.authorDate: Jean-Jacques Rousseau · 1750 (Discours sur les sciences et les arts, Geneva)
works.form: Polemical-philosophical Discourse · French Enlightenment / counter-Enlightenment
works.tagline: The progress of the sciences and arts has not improved morals — it has corrupted them, and the simplicity of pre-civilised humanity is the measure against which our progress fails

works.title: Julie (Mature (the literary high-point of Rousseau's career, between Social Contract and Émile))
works.authorDate: Jean-Jacques Rousseau · 1761 (Julie, ou la Nouvelle Héloïse: Lettres de deux amants, habitants d'une petite ville au pied des Alpes, Amsterdam)
works.form: Epistolary novel in six parts · French Enlightenment literature / pre-Romantic sensibility
works.tagline: Natural feeling against social artifice — the love of Julie and Saint-Preux, frustrated by the order of society, retains its truth at the level of the heart

works.title: Reveries of the Solitary Walker (Last (composed in Rousseau's final two years, after he had retreated from public life))
works.authorDate: Jean-Jacques Rousseau · 1776-78 (unfinished at Rousseau's death; published posthumously 1782)
works.form: Meditative essays / philosophical autobiography · French Enlightenment / pre-Romantic introspection
works.tagline: Ten meditations from solitary walks — the last and most introspective of Rousseau's autobiographical works, written when he had given up hope of public reconciliation

works.title: Epistulae Morales ad Lucilium (Late (Seneca's last completed major work, composed in retirement))
works.authorDate: Lucius Annaeus Seneca · 63-65 CE (Seneca's last years, after retirement from Nero's court and before his forced suicide)
works.form: Philosophical letters (124 surviving, originally many more) · Roman Stoicism
works.tagline: Moral philosophy in epistolary form — the daily practice of Stoic virtue, illustrated through letters of advice to a younger friend

works.title: De Beneficiis (Mid-mature (composed during Seneca's most influential political-philosophical period))
works.authorDate: Lucius Annaeus Seneca · c. 56-62 CE (Nero's court, before Seneca's retirement)
works.form: Seven-book philosophical treatise · Roman Stoicism
works.tagline: The giving and receiving of benefits is the fabric of human society — the proper conduct of benefits binds the social order together

works.title: Naturales Quaestiones (Late)
works.authorDate: Lucius Annaeus Seneca · c. 62-64 CE (composed during Seneca's retirement)
works.form: Seven-book natural-philosophical treatise · Roman Stoicism / ancient natural philosophy
works.tagline: Stoic natural philosophy as a path to wisdom — the study of meteorology, geography, and cosmology trains the mind to apprehend the rational order of the cosmos

works.title: De Otio (Late)
works.authorDate: Lucius Annaeus Seneca · c. 62 CE (composed at the time of Seneca's retirement from Nero's court)
works.form: Short philosophical treatise (incomplete — opening and closing portions lost) · Roman Stoicism
works.tagline: Philosophical retirement is not desertion of duty — it is a different mode of serving the cosmic commonwealth

works.title: De Constantia Sapientis (Mid)
works.authorDate: Lucius Annaeus Seneca · c. 56 CE (early in Seneca's tenure as Nero's advisor)
works.form: Short philosophical treatise · Roman Stoicism
works.tagline: The wise man cannot be injured because his good is what cannot be taken from him; he cannot be insulted because no insult reaches what he is

works.title: Rhetoric (Mature)
works.authorDate: Aristotle · c. 350-330 BC (composed during Aristotle's mature Lyceum period)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in three books · Classical Greek philosophy / rhetorical theory
works.tagline: Persuasion has three modes — through character (ethos), through emotion (pathos), and through reasoning (logos) — and the Rhetoric analyses each in systematic detail

works.title: Poetics (Mature)
works.authorDate: Aristotle · c. 335 BC (composed during Aristotle's Lyceum period; only the book on tragedy and epic survives; the book on comedy is lost)
works.form: Short philosophical treatise (Book 1 on tragedy surviving; Book 2 on comedy lost) · Classical Greek philosophy / literary theory
works.tagline: Tragedy is the imitation of a serious action — its proper effect is catharsis of pity and fear, achieved through the structure of plot, character, thought, diction, melody, and spectacle

works.title: Topics (Mid-mature)
works.authorDate: Aristotle · c. 350-340 BC (one of Aristotle's earlier mature logical works)
works.form: Logical treatise in eight books · Classical Greek philosophy / dialectical logic
works.tagline: Dialectical reasoning from probable premises — distinct from demonstrative syllogism — has its own proper structure and uses, foundational for philosophical and rhetorical argument

works.title: Historia Animalium (Mature)
works.authorDate: Aristotle · c. 343-340 BC (composed during Aristotle's Lesbos period and continued at the Lyceum)
works.form: Natural-historical treatise in ten books (Book 10 of disputed authenticity) · Classical Greek philosophy / ancient biology
works.tagline: The founding work of systematic biology — comparative anatomy, behaviour, reproduction, and ecology across the animal kingdom

works.title: On Generation and Corruption (Mature)
works.authorDate: Aristotle · c. 350 BC (during Aristotle's mature Lyceum period)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in two books · Classical Greek philosophy / ancient natural philosophy
works.tagline: The philosophy of substantial change — how does one thing become another? Through the four elements, their transformations, and the underlying matter that persists through them

works.title: The Mandarins (Mature)
works.form: Novel · Twentieth-century French existentialist literature
works.tagline: The post-Liberation Parisian intellectual scene confronts the cold war — through three central characters modeled on Camus, Sartre, and Beauvoir herself

works.title: Memoirs of a Dutiful Daughter (Mature)
works.form: Autobiography · Twentieth-century French existentialist autobiography
works.tagline: A young Parisian woman's break with Catholic-bourgeois upbringing and discovery of philosophical-existential vocation

works.title: Old Age (Late-mature)
works.form: Philosophical-sociological treatise · Twentieth-century French existentialism / sociology of ageing
works.tagline: Old age as a social condition systematically denied and degraded — what The Second Sex did for womanhood, La Vieillesse does for ageing

works.title: A Very Easy Death (Late)
works.form: Memoir · Twentieth-century French existentialist memoir
works.tagline: A daughter's unsparing account of her mother's six-week dying — and an indictment of the medicalised lies that surrounded it

works.title: Orlando (Mature)
works.form: Mock-biographical novel · Twentieth-century English modernist literature
works.tagline: A young Elizabethan nobleman lives through four centuries and changes sex midway — founding work of modernist gender-fluid literature

works.title: The Waves (Mature)
works.form: Modernist novel in six interlocking soliloquies · Twentieth-century English modernist literature
works.tagline: Six characters, six soliloquies, six lives from childhood to death — Woolf's most formally experimental novel and her own choice as her best work

works.title: Three Guineas (Late)
works.form: Polemical-philosophical extended essay · Twentieth-century English feminist political philosophy
works.tagline: How can we prevent war? An anti-fascist feminist argues the patriarchal-educational establishment and the war system are continuous

works.title: Between the Acts (Last)
works.authorDate: Virginia Woolf · 1940-41 (Hogarth, posthumous July 1941; Woolf died March 28, 1941)
works.form: Novel · Twentieth-century English modernist literature
works.tagline: A village pageant on a June day in 1939 — England's historical-cultural reckoning at the brink of the Second World War

works.title: Gyn/Ecology (Mature)
works.form: Radical-feminist philosophical-theological treatise · Twentieth-century radical feminism / post-Christian feminist philosophy
works.tagline: Patriarchy is a global religion of dismemberment — sati, foot-binding, genital mutilation are its rituals; gyn/ecology is the women-centred ecology that resists

works.title: Pure Lust (Late-mature)
works.form: Radical-feminist philosophical treatise · Twentieth-century radical feminism
works.tagline: "Pure Lust" — elemental passion of women's desire for life, freedom, creative communion, against the "phallic lust" of patriarchal possession

works.title: Japji Sahib (Mature (Nānak's foundational devotional composition))
works.authorDate: Guru Nānak Dev Ji · c. 1499-1539 (during Nānak's later teaching years; the morning prayer is one of his foundational compositions)
works.form: Devotional poetic prayer (38 pauris/stanzas plus a closing salok) · Sikhism
works.tagline: There is one God, the eternal truth — Ik Onkar Sat Nam — known by direct devotional remembrance rather than by ritual or priestly mediation

works.title: Asa Di Var (Mature)
works.authorDate: Guru Nānak Dev Ji · c. 1500-1539 (Nānak's mature teaching years; included in the Guru Granth Sahib 1604)
works.form: Devotional ballad (var) in 24 pauris with shaloks · Sikhism
works.tagline: A morning ballad in Asa raga — Nānak's extended devotional-philosophical reflection on God, creation, humility, and the ethical life

works.title: Veritatis Splendor (Mature)
works.authorDate: Karol Józef Wojtyła / Pope John Paul II · 1993 (Veritatis Splendor, issued August 6, 1993)
works.form: Papal encyclical · Catholic moral theology / Christian personalism
works.tagline: The splendour of truth shines in every action of the human being who is open to truth — moral truth has objective foundations, and the freedom that severs from truth becomes its own corruption

works.title: Evangelium Vitae (Late-mature)
works.authorDate: Karol Józef Wojtyła / Pope John Paul II · 1995 (Evangelium Vitae, issued March 25, 1995, the feast of the Annunciation)
works.form: Papal encyclical · Catholic moral theology / Christian personalism
works.tagline: The Gospel of Life — the inviolable dignity of human life from conception to natural death, against the "culture of death" that organises modern society against it

works.title: Theology of the Body (Mature (the major catechetical project of John Paul II's early pontificate))
works.authorDate: Karol Józef Wojtyła / Pope John Paul II · 1979-84 (129 Wednesday General Audience addresses; published collectively as Man and Woman He Created Them: A Theology of the Body)
works.form: Series of Wednesday General Audience addresses · Catholic moral theology / Christian personalism / theology of marriage
works.tagline: The body is the original sacrament — the visible sign of the invisible love that makes marriage, celibacy, and human sexuality the proper expressions of the human person

works.title: Politics and Conscience (Mature (composed during Havel's dissident period before the 1989 Velvet Revolution))
works.authorDate: Václav Havel · 1984 (composed in Czechoslovakia under Communist authority; prepared as the acceptance speech for an honorary degree from the University of Toulouse that Havel could not attend)
works.form: Philosophical-political essay · Twentieth-century Czech philosophical dissidence
works.tagline: Modern political crisis has spiritual roots — and the recovery of political life requires the recovery of the inner conditions of authentic action

works.title: Disturbing the Peace (Late-dissident (composed three years before the Velvet Revolution))
works.authorDate: Václav Havel · 1985-86 (long interview composed by mail between Havel in Prague and Karel Hvížďala in West Germany)
works.form: Long autobiographical-political interview · Twentieth-century Czech philosophical dissidence
works.tagline: A long interview through which Havel surveys his life, his philosophy, and his political commitments in the years just before the 1989 Velvet Revolution

works.title: Summer Meditations (Late (the first major post-1989 reflection on the transition from dissidence to governance))
works.authorDate: Václav Havel · 1991 (Letní přemítání, composed during Havel's first eighteen months as Czechoslovak president after the November 1989 Velvet Revolution)
works.form: Essay collection · Twentieth-century Czech philosophical dissidence / post-Communist political philosophy
works.tagline: Reflections from a dissident now in the presidential office — on the moral-political transition from "living in truth" to actual democratic governance

works.title: Romans (Mature (Paul's most extensive and systematic letter))
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 56-58 CE (composed in Corinth, near the end of Paul's third missionary journey)
works.form: Pastoral-doctrinal letter (16 chapters) · Earliest Christianity / Pauline Christianity
works.tagline: Justification by faith, the universality of sin, the universality of grace, and the place of Israel in God's redemptive plan — Paul's most systematic theological exposition

works.title: 1 Corinthians (Mature)
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 53-55 CE (composed in Ephesus during Paul's third missionary journey)
works.form: Pastoral letter (16 chapters) · Earliest Christianity / Pauline Christianity
works.tagline: A pastoral letter to a troubled church — addressing factions, immorality, lawsuits, marriage, food offered to idols, worship, spiritual gifts, the resurrection, and the famous "hymn to love" of chapter 13

works.title: 2 Corinthians (Mature)
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 55-56 CE (composed in Macedonia after a difficult Corinthian crisis)
works.form: Pastoral-apologetic letter (13 chapters) · Earliest Christianity / Pauline Christianity
works.tagline: When I am weak, then I am strong — Paul's most personal letter, defending his apostolic ministry through the theology of weakness

works.title: Galatians (Mature)
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 48-55 CE (either earliest or middle Pauline letter)
works.form: Polemical pastoral letter (6 chapters) · Earliest Christianity / Pauline Christianity
works.tagline: Justification by faith, not by works of the law — Paul's polemical manifesto, the "Magna Carta" of Christian freedom

works.title: Philippians (Late)
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 60-62 CE (from prison — Rome, Ephesus, or Caesarea)
works.form: Pastoral letter (4 chapters) · Earliest Christianity / Pauline Christianity
works.tagline: A prison letter of joy — and the Christological kenosis hymn that has organised Christian doctrine of incarnation for two millennia

works.title: 1 Thessalonians (Early)
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 50-51 CE (earliest surviving Pauline letter)
works.form: Pastoral letter (5 chapters) · Earliest Christianity
works.tagline: The earliest surviving Pauline letter — eschatological questions about the parousia and the dead in Christ

works.title: Philemon (Late)
works.authorDate: Paul of Tarsus (Saul / Saint Paul) · c. 60-62 CE (composed in prison alongside Colossians)
works.form: Short personal letter (25 verses) · Earliest Christianity
works.tagline: Paul's short prison letter on behalf of a runaway slave — and the principal early-Christian text on slavery, manumission, and the gospel's social implications

works.title: Othello (Mature)
works.authorDate: William Shakespeare · c. 1603-04 (first performed Whitehall, 1 November 1604)
works.form: Tragic drama in five acts · Elizabethan-Jacobean English Renaissance drama
works.tagline: Jealousy, race, and the destruction of a good man — Iago's motiveless malignity working through Othello's capacity to be deceived

works.title: Macbeth (Mature)
works.form: Tragic drama in five acts · Elizabethan-Jacobean English Renaissance drama
works.tagline: Ambition, regicide, and the destruction of conscience — Shakespeare's most concentrated tragic structure

works.title: Antony and Cleopatra (Mature)
works.form: Tragic drama in five acts · Elizabethan-Jacobean English Renaissance drama
works.tagline: Love and empire — the fall of Antony and Cleopatra and the end of the Roman Republic

works.title: The Tempest (Last (probably Shakespeare's last sole-authored play))
works.authorDate: William Shakespeare · c. 1610-11 (first performed Whitehall, 1 November 1611)
works.form: Late romance in five acts · Elizabethan-Jacobean English Renaissance drama
works.tagline: Prospero on his enchanted island — magic of theatrical art, civilisation and colonisation, Shakespeare's farewell to the stage

works.title: Measure for Measure (Mature)
works.form: Problem comedy / dark comedy in five acts · Elizabethan-Jacobean English Renaissance drama
works.tagline: "Measure for measure" — a deeply ambiguous drama of sexual ethics, judicial power, and the relation between mercy and justice

works.title: Resistance to Civil Government (Mature)
works.authorDate: Henry David Thoreau · 1849 (first published as "Resistance to Civil Government" in Aesthetic Papers; reprinted posthumously as "Civil Disobedience" in 1866)
works.form: Political-philosophical essay · American transcendentalism / political-philosophical individualism
works.tagline: "That government is best which governs least" — and when government becomes unjust, refusal to obey is not only a right but a duty

works.title: A Week on the Concord and Merrimack Rivers (Early-mature)
works.authorDate: Henry David Thoreau · 1849 (composed during Thoreau's Walden Pond years 1845-47; published 1849 at Thoreau's own expense)
works.form: Travel memoir / philosophical essay · American transcendentalism
works.tagline: A boat trip up the Concord and Merrimack rivers — and the philosophical-poetic-religious meditations the trip occasions

works.title: The Maine Woods (Mature-late)
works.authorDate: Henry David Thoreau · 1846-57 (three Maine expedition narratives composed across a decade); compiled posthumously 1864
works.form: Wilderness travel narrative (three linked accounts) · American transcendentalism / wilderness literature
works.tagline: Three Maine expeditions — Ktaadn, Chesuncook, the Allegash and East Branch — and the foundational American confrontation with the unmodified wilderness

works.title: A Plea for Captain John Brown (Mature)
works.authorDate: Henry David Thoreau · 1859 (delivered as a public address in Concord, Boston, and Worcester, October-November 1859; published 1860)
works.form: Political address / polemical essay · American transcendentalism / radical abolitionism
works.tagline: A defense of John Brown after Harpers Ferry — the most politically incendiary of Thoreau's essays

works.title: Slavery in Massachusetts (Mature)
works.authorDate: Henry David Thoreau · 1854 (delivered at the antislavery convention, Framingham, July 4, 1854; published in The Liberator and other papers)
works.form: Political address · American transcendentalism / radical abolitionism
works.tagline: Massachusetts has become complicit in slavery through the Fugitive Slave Act — and the citizens who tolerate this complicity have themselves become slave-catchers

works.title: The American Scholar (Mature)
works.authorDate: Ralph Waldo Emerson · 1837 (delivered August 31, 1837, at the Phi Beta Kappa Society of Harvard; first published as An Oration Delivered before the Phi Beta Kappa Society, 1837)
works.form: Public address · American transcendentalism
works.tagline: America's intellectual Declaration of Independence — the scholar's vocation, the proper relation between books and life

works.title: Divinity School Address (Mature)
works.authorDate: Ralph Waldo Emerson · 1838 (delivered July 15, 1838, at Harvard Divinity School; published as An Address Delivered before the Senior Class in Divinity College, Cambridge, 1838)
works.form: Public address · American transcendentalism / liberal religion
works.tagline: The miracle is not Christ's but the ongoing miracle of moral-religious sensibility in every soul — Emerson's founding manifesto of transcendentalist religion

works.title: Essays: First Series (Mature)
works.authorDate: Ralph Waldo Emerson · 1841 (James Munroe & Co., Boston)
works.form: Essay collection (12 essays) · American transcendentalism
works.tagline: Self-Reliance, Compensation, The Over-Soul — the most concentrated statement of Emerson's transcendentalist programme

works.title: Essays: Second Series (Mature)
works.authorDate: Ralph Waldo Emerson · 1844 (James Munroe & Co., Boston)
works.form: Essay collection (8 essays) · American transcendentalism
works.tagline: The poet, experience, nature, politics — Emerson's second collection, more sober and philosophically mature than the First Series

works.title: Representative Men (Mature)
works.authorDate: Ralph Waldo Emerson · 1850 (Phillips, Sampson & Co., Boston; based on lectures delivered 1845-46)
works.form: Biographical-philosophical essay collection · American transcendentalism
works.tagline: Plato, Swedenborg, Montaigne, Shakespeare, Napoleon, Goethe — Emerson's engagement with the great figures of Western thought as representative types of human possibility

works.title: Soliloquies (Early)
works.form: Philosophical-religious meditations (5 monologues) · German Romanticism / liberal Protestant theology
works.tagline: Five meditations on selfhood, freedom, world, prospect, and youth — Schleiermacher's founding statement of ethical individuality

works.title: Brief Outline of Theology as a Field of Study (Mature)
works.authorDate: Friedrich Schleiermacher · 1811 (first edition); substantially revised 1830 (second edition)
works.form: Theological-methodological treatise · Liberal Protestant theology / nineteenth-century German theology
works.tagline: Theology as a positive science — Schleiermacher's methodological treatise organising the field into philosophical, historical, and practical theology

works.title: Translation of Plato's dialogues (Mature)
works.authorDate: Friedrich Schleiermacher · 1804-28 (multi-volume translation with extensive prefaces and notes)
works.form: Translation with critical introductions · German classical philology / nineteenth-century Platonic scholarship
works.tagline: The founding modern German translation of Plato — Schleiermacher's philological achievement that reshaped nineteenth-century Plato studies

works.title: The Christian Faith (Mature)
works.authorDate: Friedrich Schleiermacher · 1821-22 (first edition); substantially revised 1830-31 (second edition, the standard form)
works.form: Systematic dogmatic theology · Liberal Protestant theology
works.tagline: The founding modern Protestant systematic theology — Schleiermacher's reconstruction of Christian doctrine on the basis of religious self-consciousness

works.title: Surprised by Joy (Late-mature)
works.form: Spiritual autobiography · Twentieth-century Anglican apologetics
works.tagline: Lewis's autobiographical account of his conversion — the role of imagination, longing (Joy), and reason in the path from atheism to Christianity

works.title: The Allegory of Love (Mature)
works.authorDate: C. S. Lewis · 1936 (Oxford UP); Hawthornden Prize 1936
works.form: Literary-historical scholarship · Twentieth-century English medieval-Renaissance literary scholarship
works.tagline: Courtly love, allegorical poetry, the medieval European literary tradition — Lewis's scholarly masterpiece on the literary form that organised European love poetry from the troubadours to Spenser

works.title: The Discarded Image (Last)
works.authorDate: C. S. Lewis · Lectures delivered Oxford 1950s; published posthumously 1964 (Cambridge UP)
works.form: Literary-historical introduction · Twentieth-century English medieval-Renaissance literary scholarship
works.tagline: The medieval cosmological model — the discarded image of the world that organised pre-modern European literary-cultural life

works.title: Sartre: Romantic Rationalist (Early)
works.form: Philosophical-critical study · Twentieth-century British philosophy / engagement with French existentialism
works.tagline: Iris Murdoch's 1953 short study of Sartre — her first published book and the founding statement of her philosophical-literary engagement with French existentialism

works.title: The Sea, The Sea (Late-mature)
works.form: Novel · Twentieth-century British philosophical fiction
works.tagline: A retired theatrical director's seaside memoir — and the metaphysical study of obsession, jealousy, and self-deception that the memoir becomes

works.title: The Black Prince (Mature)
works.authorDate: Iris Murdoch · 1973 (Chatto & Windus); James Tait Black Memorial Prize 1973
works.form: Novel · Twentieth-century British philosophical fiction
works.tagline: Bradley Pearson, an aging writer, encounters Julian Baffin and is overwhelmed by erotic-philosophical love — Murdoch's most ambitious novel on Eros and the artistic vocation

works.title: A Pluralistic Universe (Late)
works.authorDate: William James · 1909 (Hibbert Lectures at Manchester College, Oxford, May 1908)
works.form: Philosophical lecture series · American pragmatism / radical empiricism
works.tagline: The universe is plural, not monistic — James's mature constructive metaphysics against absolute idealism

works.title: Essays in Radical Empiricism (Late posthumous)
works.form: Philosophical essay collection · American pragmatism / radical empiricism
works.tagline: Radical empiricism — experience as foundation of metaphysics, with relations as much given as things related

works.title: Some Remarks on Logical Form (Transitional)
works.authorDate: Ludwig Wittgenstein · 1929 (Proceedings of the Aristotelian Society, Supplementary Volume 9)
works.form: Philosophical paper (short) · Twentieth-century analytic philosophy of logic
works.tagline: Wittgenstein's only short published paper — on color-exclusion and the limits of the Tractarian system

works.title: Eyeless in Gaza (Mid-mature)
works.form: Novel (non-linear) · Twentieth-century English novel / spiritual-pacifist literature
works.tagline: Huxley's formally experimental 1936 novel — path from cynical bohemianism to mystical-pacifist commitment

works.title: Brave New World Revisited (Late)
works.form: Essay collection · Twentieth-century political-cultural criticism
works.tagline: Twenty-six years later — Huxley's sober reassessment argues the dystopia is arriving faster than he had imagined

works.title: Purgatorio (Mature)
works.form: Epic poem in 33 cantos of terza rima · Late medieval Italian Catholic poetry
works.tagline: The ascent of Mount Purgatory through the seven terraces of capital vice — Dante's most theologically careful cantica

works.title: De Vulgari Eloquentia (Mid-mature)
works.authorDate: Dante Alighieri · c. 1304-05 (two of four planned books)
works.form: Linguistic-rhetorical treatise (incomplete) · Late medieval Italian philosophy of language
works.tagline: The founding philosophical treatise on vernacular language — Dante defending the dignity of Italian against Latin while writing in Latin

works.title: De Monarchia (Late)
works.form: Political-philosophical treatise in three books · Late medieval political philosophy
works.tagline: Universal monarchy as the proper political form for humanity — and the imperial-papal independence

works.title: The Descent of Man (Mature)
works.form: Scientific treatise in two volumes · Nineteenth-century evolutionary biology
works.tagline: Humans as the product of evolutionary descent — and the theory of sexual selection as a major mechanism of evolutionary change

works.title: The Expression of the Emotions in Man and Animals (Late)
works.form: Scientific treatise · Nineteenth-century evolutionary biology / psychology
works.tagline: Comparative emotional expression — Darwin's foundational study of facial expression across humans and animals, with photographs of emotional states

works.title: Journal of Researches (Early)
works.authorDate: Charles Darwin · 1839 (first edition); 1845 (substantially revised second edition)
works.form: Scientific-naturalist travel narrative · Nineteenth-century natural history / scientific autobiography
works.tagline: Darwin's account of his 1831-36 Beagle voyage — the formative experience of his evolutionary thinking, popularly known as "The Voyage of the Beagle"

works.title: The Formation of Vegetable Mould through the Action of Worms (Last)
works.authorDate: Charles Darwin · 1881 (John Murray, London) — Darwin's last book, published months before his April 1882 death
works.form: Scientific monograph · Nineteenth-century natural history / soil biology
works.tagline: Earthworms as geological agents — Darwin's last book, the founding text of soil biology and ecological-functional ecology

works.title: The Variation of Animals and Plants under Domestication (Mature)
works.form: Scientific treatise in two volumes · Nineteenth-century evolutionary biology
works.tagline: Domestic variation and the now-discarded theory of pangenesis — Darwin's major treatment of inheritance, ten years after Origin

works.title: Relativity: The Special and the General Theory (Mid-mature)
works.authorDate: Albert Einstein · 1916 (Über die spezielle und die allgemeine Relativitätstheorie); English translation 1920
works.form: Popular-scientific exposition · Twentieth-century physics / philosophy of physics
works.tagline: Einstein's own popular exposition of special and general relativity — the principal accessible source for the lay reader

works.title: The World as I See It (Mid-mature)
works.authorDate: Albert Einstein · 1934 (German: Mein Weltbild, Querido Verlag, Amsterdam; English: Covici Friede, New York)
works.form: Essay and address collection · Twentieth-century scientific humanism / philosophy of science
works.tagline: Einstein's 1934 collection of essays and addresses — philosophical, political, personal — the most accessible non-technical statement of his worldview

works.title: Religion and Science (Mid-mature)
works.authorDate: Albert Einstein · 1930 (published New York Times Magazine, November 9, 1930)
works.form: Short essay · Twentieth-century scientific humanism / philosophy of religion
works.tagline: Three stages of religion — fear, moral, cosmic — and mature science as continuous with cosmic religious feeling

works.title: Out of My Later Years (Late)
works.form: Essay and address collection · Twentieth-century scientific humanism / philosophy of science / philosophy of religion
works.tagline: Einstein's 1950 late-career essay collection — Princeton years on science, politics, religion, and Zionism

works.title: The Born-Einstein Letters (Mature-late)
works.authorDate: Albert Einstein · 1916-55 (correspondence across four decades); published in 1971 (German); English 1971 (Walker)
works.form: Scientific correspondence · Twentieth-century physics / philosophy of science
works.tagline: The Einstein-Born letters — the principal documentary record of the early-twentieth-century physics debate, especially over quantum mechanics

works.title: Letter to Herodotus (Mature)
works.form: Philosophical letter · Hellenistic philosophy / Epicureanism
works.tagline: Epicurus's short summary of his natural philosophy — atoms, void, infinite worlds

works.title: Principal Doctrines (Mature)
works.form: Collection of 40 philosophical maxims · Hellenistic philosophy / Epicureanism
works.tagline: 40 short Epicurean maxims — most-quoted statement of Epicurean ethics

works.title: Parable of the Sower (Mature)
works.form: Novel (epistolary-journal form) · African American speculative fiction / Afrofuturism
works.tagline: Near-future America collapsing into climate breakdown — and Lauren Olamina's Earthseed religion

works.title: Parable of the Talents (Late-mature)
works.form: Novel (multi-voiced journal) · African American speculative fiction / Afrofuturism
works.tagline: Earthseed community attacked by a theocratic American president — "Make America Great Again" predating Trump by 17 years

works.title: The Immeasurable Equation (Posthumous)
works.form: Poetry and prose collection · Afrofuturism
works.tagline: Collected poetic-philosophical writings — foundational text of Afrofuturism

works.title: Cur Deus Homo (Late-mature)
works.form: Philosophical dialogue · Medieval Latin theology / scholasticism
works.tagline: Why God became man — Anselm's rational argument for the necessity of the Incarnation, founding text of satisfaction theory of atonement

works.title: On Truth (Mature)
works.form: Philosophical dialogue · Medieval Latin theology
works.tagline: Truth as "rectitude" — Anselm's philosophical analysis of truth in propositions, opinions, will, action, sensation, and substantial being

works.title: On Free Will (Mature)
works.form: Philosophical dialogue · Medieval Latin theology
works.tagline: Free will as the power to preserve rightness of will for its own sake — Anselm's distinctive medieval definition

works.title: Faust I (Mature)
works.authorDate: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe · 1772-1806 (composed over 35 years; published 1808)
works.form: Verse drama in two parts (Part I) · German Romanticism / classical-modern European drama
works.tagline: Faust's pact with Mephistopheles — the founding text of the Faust mythos in modern European literature

works.title: Faust II (Last)
works.authorDate: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe · 1825-31 (completed shortly before Goethe's 1832 death; published posthumously 1832)
works.form: Verse drama in five acts · German Romanticism / late classical modernism
works.tagline: Goethe's 1832 posthumous Faust II — Faust's journey through history, the salvation of his striving soul

works.title: Theory of Colors (Mature)
works.authorDate: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe · 1810 (J.G. Cotta, Tübingen)
works.form: Phenomenological-scientific treatise · German Romanticism / phenomenology of perception
works.tagline: Goethe's phenomenological treatise on color — long out of favour against Newton, rehabilitated by twentieth-century phenomenology

works.title: Italian Journey (Late-mature retrospective)
works.authorDate: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe · 1816 (parts I-II) and 1829 (part III); recounting 1786-88 journey
works.form: Travel memoir / autobiographical-aesthetic essay · German classical-Romantic literature
works.tagline: Goethe's account of his 1786-88 Italian journey — the formative classical experience that reshaped his aesthetic-philosophical vision

works.title: Discourse on Metaphysics (Mature)
works.authorDate: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz · 1686 (composed February 1686; first published 1846)
works.form: Philosophical treatise in 37 sections · Early modern philosophy / German rationalism
works.tagline: Leibniz's 1686 systematic statement of his mature metaphysics — initiating the famous Arnauld correspondence

works.title: The Leibniz-Clarke Correspondence (Last)
works.authorDate: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz · 1715-16 (5 letters from Leibniz, 5 replies from Clarke); published 1717
works.form: Philosophical correspondence · Early modern philosophy of physics
works.tagline: Leibniz's 1715-16 last philosophical work — the founding debate over space, time, and divine action between relationalist and absolutist physics

works.title: Jesus and the Disinherited (Mature)
works.form: Theological-philosophical essay · Twentieth-century African American liberal-prophetic Christianity
works.tagline: Jesus as a disinherited Palestinian Jew — Thurman's short book that shaped Martin Luther King Jr. and the civil rights movement

works.title: The Inward Journey (Late-mature)
works.form: Meditational essays · Twentieth-century African American contemplative Christianity
works.tagline: The inward journey — Thurman's mature spiritual-contemplative teaching synthesising Black prophetic Christianity, Quaker contemplation, Indian-Buddhist practice

works.title: Go Tell It on the Mountain (Early)
works.form: Novel · African American literature
works.tagline: Baldwin's semi-autobiographical first novel — Harlem Pentecostal-Christian boyhood

works.title: Notes of a Native Son (Mid-mature)
works.form: Essay collection (10 essays) · African American literary-political essay
works.tagline: Baldwin's founding essay collection — title essay on his father, "Stranger in the Village," Paris pieces

works.title: Giovanni's Room (Mid-mature)
works.form: Novel · African American literature / mid-twentieth-century LGBT literature
works.tagline: Baldwin's 1956 Paris novel of male homosexual love — major founding text of post-war LGBT literature

works.title: A Madman's Diary (Mid-mature)
works.form: Short story (diary form) · Modern Chinese literature / May Fourth
works.tagline: Lu Xun's 1918 founding text — first major work in vernacular Chinese, indicting Confucian society as "cannibalistic"

works.title: The True Story of Ah Q (Mature)
works.form: Novella · Modern Chinese literature / May Fourth
works.tagline: The great satirical portrait of the Chinese national character — Ah Q's "spiritual victory" psychology

works.title: The Sea of Fertility (Last)
works.form: Tetralogy of four novels · Twentieth-century Japanese literature
works.tagline: Mishima's final tetralogy — completed the day of his 1970 ritual suicide, tracing reincarnation across twentieth-century Japan

works.title: Patriotism (Mid-mature)
works.form: Short story · Twentieth-century Japanese literature
works.tagline: Mishima's 1961 short story of a young officer's ritual suicide after the 1936 coup — prefiguring his own 1970 death

works.title: Slavery and Freedom (Late-mature)

works.title: "Relative State" Formulation of Quantum Mechanics
works.form: Doctoral dissertation / journal article (Reviews of Modern Physics) · Quantum foundations / philosophy of physics
works.tagline: No wave function collapse — the universal wave function branches, and every outcome is realised in a relative state

works.title: Fragments and Testimonia
works.authorDate: Protagoras of Abdera · c. 5th century BCE (fragments preserved in Plato, Sextus Empiricus, Diogenes Laertius)
works.form: Fragments and testimonia (reconstructed) · Sophistic philosophy / Presocratic epistemology
works.tagline: Man is the measure of all things — the founding statement of Western relativism and the first professional educator of Greece

works.title: Food of the Gods
works.form: Speculative cultural history · Psychedelic philosophy / ethnobotany
works.tagline: The "stoned ape" thesis — psychedelic mushrooms catalysed the emergence of human consciousness, language, and culture

works.title: Instructions for Practical Living
works.authorDate: Wang Yangming (Wang Shouren) · c. 1518 (compiled by students; expanded editions to 1572)
works.form: Dialogues and philosophical letters compiled by students · Neo-Confucian / Yangming school (School of Mind)
works.tagline: Knowledge and action are one — the mind itself is principle (xin ji li), and innate knowing (liangzhi) needs no external verification

works.title: Letter to Demetrias
works.form: Theological letter / moral exhortation · Late antique Latin Christianity / Pelagianism
works.tagline: Human nature is good, free will is real, and sinlessness is possible — the anti-Augustinian case for moral optimism

works.title: On Behalf of the Fool
works.form: Philosophical counter-argument / critical reply · Medieval Latin theology / Benedictine monasticism
works.tagline: The perfect island objection — if Anselm's argument proves God exists, it proves everything perfect exists, which is absurd

works.title: Xunzi
works.form: Collected philosophical essays (32 chapters) · Confucian philosophy / Warring States period
works.tagline: Human nature is evil — but ritual, education, and the accumulated wisdom of the sage-kings can transform it into goodness

works.title: Mozi
works.authorDate: Mozi (Mo Di) and followers · c. 5th century BCE (with later additions)
works.form: Collected philosophical essays (71 chapters, 53 surviving) · Mohist philosophy / Warring States period
works.tagline: Universal love for all people without distinction — the anti-Confucian, anti-war, proto-utilitarian philosopher of ancient China

works.title: The Spiritual Medicine
works.form: Ethical treatise in twenty chapters · Islamic philosophy / practical ethics
works.tagline: Reason as the cure for the passions — Platonic-Galenic soul-medicine without prophecy

works.title: Hayy ibn Yaqzan
works.form: Philosophical romance / allegorical narrative · Islamic philosophy (falsafa)
works.tagline: The solitary child who reasons his way from nature to God — the sufficiency of unaided intellect

works.title: Indica
works.form: Systematic encyclopaedic treatise in eighty chapters · Islamic scholarship / comparative study of religions and sciences
works.tagline: The impartial observer lets India speak — religion, philosophy, science, and society mapped with empirical fairness

works.title: The Book of Beliefs and Opinions
works.form: Systematic theological treatise in ten chapters · Jewish philosophy / kalam theology
works.tagline: Reason and revelation as twin witnesses to the same truth — Judaism's first systematic theology

works.title: The Kuzari
works.form: Philosophical dialogue in five parts · Jewish philosophy / anti-Aristotelian apologetics
works.tagline: The God of Abraham over the God of Aristotle — historical testimony and lived experience as the foundation of faith

works.title: Commentary on the Torah
works.form: Continuous verse-by-verse biblical commentary · Rabbinic Judaism / Kabbalistic exegesis
works.tagline: The Torah is composed of divine Names — beneath the literal surface lies an infinite mystical depth

works.title: Fons Vitae
works.form: Philosophical dialogue in five treatises between master and disciple · Jewish Neoplatonism / Latin scholasticism
works.tagline: All beings from angels to stones are composed of matter and form — the universal hylomorphism that divided the schoolmen

works.title: On Loving God (De Diligendo Deo)
works.form: Treatise-letter, addressed to Cardinal Haimeric · Cistercian mystical theology
works.tagline: The measure of loving God is to love without measure — the soul's ascent through four degrees of love

works.title: On Divine Omnipotence (De Divina Omnipotentia)
works.form: Letter-treatise (Epistola 119), addressed to Abbot Desiderius of Monte Cassino · Monastic theology / pre-scholastic philosophy of religion
works.tagline: Can God undo the past? — the first systematic medieval argument that divine omnipotence exceeds the reach of logic

works.title: De Animalibus
works.form: Encyclopedic paraphrase and commentary in 26 books · Aristotelian natural history / Dominican scholasticism
works.tagline: Experiment alone certifies in these things — the Universal Doctor observes nature on its own terms

works.title: Itinerarium Mentis in Deum (The Journey of the Mind into God)
works.form: Short mystical-philosophical treatise in seven chapters · Franciscan theology / Augustinian-Dionysian mysticism
works.tagline: Ask grace, not doctrine — the soul's six-winged ascent from creatures to the ecstatic transitus beyond all knowing

works.title: On Light (De Luce)
works.form: Short philosophical treatise (opusculum) · Oxford natural philosophy / Augustinian light-metaphysics
works.tagline: Light is the first corporeal form — the cosmos generated by the infinite self-multiplication of an original point

works.title: The Imitation of Christ (De Imitatione Christi)
works.form: Devotional treatise in four books · Devotio Moderna / Augustinian spirituality
works.tagline: I would rather feel compunction than know how to define it — interior devotion as the one thing needful

works.title: Against Aristotle on the Eternity of the World
works.form: Philosophical treatise (surviving in fragments preserved by Simplicius) · Christian Alexandrian Aristotelianism
works.tagline: The world cannot be eternal — an actually infinite past is impossible, therefore the cosmos had a beginning in time

works.title: Ambigua
works.authorDate: Maximus the Confessor · c. 628–634 CE (Ambigua ad Iohannem) and c. 614–625 CE (Ambigua ad Thomam)
works.form: Exegetical-speculative treatise in two collections (71 difficulties) · Byzantine Christian theology, patristic synthesis
works.tagline: Cosmic liturgy in seventy-one difficulties — every created thing moves from God, through Christ, back to God in theosis

works.title: Triads in Defence of the Holy Hesychasts
works.form: Three triads of discourses (nine chapters in three groups of three) · Byzantine Hesychast theology
works.tagline: The uncreated light of Tabor is not a metaphor — God's energies are truly God, truly participated, and truly seen by the saints in prayer

works.title: Chronographia
works.form: Historical narrative in seven books (later extended) · Byzantine court historiography
works.tagline: The philosopher at court — psychological portraiture, political intrigue, and the Platonic mind behind the throne of Byzantium

works.title: Attaining Enlightenment in This Very Existence
works.form: Short doctrinal treatise with scriptural citations and verse · Shingon (True Word) esoteric Buddhism
works.tagline: This very body becomes Buddha — through the Three Mysteries of body, speech, and mind, the practitioner is identical with Mahavairocana

works.title: Treatise on the Golden Lion
works.authorDate: Fazang · c. 699 CE (lecture to Empress Wu Zetian)
works.form: Short pedagogical treatise in ten chapters · Huayan (Flower Garland) Buddhism
works.tagline: The gold is in the lion, the lion is in the gold — principle and phenomena interpenetrate, and every part contains the whole

works.title: Great Calming and Contemplation
works.form: Comprehensive meditation manual in ten chapters (recorded lectures) · Tiantai Buddhism
works.tagline: Three thousand realms in a single moment of thought — calming and contemplation as the path to realising the threefold truth

works.title: Tantraloka
works.form: Encyclopaedic verse treatise in thirty-seven chapters (ahnikas) · Kashmir Shaivism (Trika)
works.tagline: All reality is the free creative pulsation of Shiva-consciousness — the Tantraloka maps every path from ignorance to recognition

works.title: Revelations (Revelationes Caelestes)
works.authorDate: Birgitta of Sweden · c. 1344–1373 (dictated over nearly thirty years; Latin translations by confessors)
works.form: Visionary compilation in eight books plus the Revelationes Extravagantes · Medieval Latin Christian visionary literature
works.tagline: Christ and the Virgin speak to a Swedish noblewoman — prophetic commands to reform the Church, recall popes from Avignon, and renew Christendom

works.title: Nyayakusumanjali
works.form: Philosophical treatise in five chapters (stabakas, "clusters of flowers") · Nyaya-Vaisheshika school of Hindu philosophy
works.tagline: Logical proof of God's existence through five arguments — the world as effect, atomic combination, cosmic order, knowledge, and scriptural authority all require Ishvara

works.title: Tattvopaplavasimha
works.form: Philosophical treatise (dialectical demolition of all pramanas) · Indian scepticism / Charvaka-Lokayata (debated)
works.tagline: A systematic demolition of every means of valid knowledge recognised by every Indian school — perception, inference, testimony, all devoured

works.title: Inquiry into the Origin of Humanity
works.form: Philosophical-doctrinal essay (panjiao — doctrinal classification) · Huayan Buddhism / Chan (Zen) Buddhism
works.tagline: From Confucianism and Daoism through Hinayana and Mahayana to the supreme truth of Original Awakening — a grand classification of all teachings

works.title: Rihla (The Travels)
works.authorDate: Ibn Battuta (dictated to Ibn Juzayy) · 1355 (dictated at the court of Abu Inan, the Marinid sultan of Fez)
works.form: Travel narrative (rihla) in two volumes · Arabic rihla (travel writing) literature
works.tagline: Seventy-five thousand miles over thirty years — the most comprehensive firsthand account of the fourteenth-century world, from Tangier to Hangzhou

works.title: Institutiones Divinarum et Saecularium Litterarum
works.form: Pedagogical handbook in two books · Late-antique Latin Christian monastic education
works.tagline: The preservation of classical learning at the end of the Roman world — a curriculum for monks who would carry civilisation through the dark centuries

works.title: Etymologiae (Origines)
works.authorDate: Isidore of Seville · c. 615–636 CE (unfinished at death; completed by Braulio of Zaragoza)
works.form: Encyclopedia in twenty books · Late-antique Latin encyclopedic tradition (Varro, Pliny)
works.tagline: The sum of classical and patristic knowledge in a single work — organised by the principle that the name of each thing reveals its nature

works.title: Pastoral Care (Regula Pastoralis)
works.authorDate: Pope Gregory I (Gregory the Great) · 591 CE
works.form: Pastoral treatise in four parts · Latin patristic pastoral theology
works.tagline: The art of arts is the government of souls — the bishop as physician who adapts his teaching to each patient's condition

works.title: Ecclesiastical History of the English People
works.form: Historical narrative in five books · Latin Christian historiography (Eusebius, Orosius)
works.tagline: The providential conversion of the English — a monk at Jarrow writes the foundational history of an island church

works.title: De Rhetorica et Virtutibus (On Rhetoric and the Virtues)
works.form: Dialogue · Carolingian educational literature; Ciceronian rhetorical tradition
works.tagline: Rhetoric and virtue in the Carolingian court — the art of persuasion as the servant of justice and Christian governance

works.title: The Ladder of Divine Ascent
works.authorDate: John Climacus (John of the Ladder) · c. 600–649 CE
works.form: Spiritual treatise in thirty steps (logoi) · Eastern Christian monasticism (Desert Fathers, Evagrius Ponticus)
works.tagline: Thirty rungs from the renunciation of the world to the ineffable summit — the foundational Eastern monastic manual of spiritual combat and contemplation

works.title: Altus Prosator
works.form: Abecedarian hymn in 23 stanzas (6 lines each) · Hiberno-Latin hymnody; Celtic monastic literature
works.tagline: The earliest surviving Irish hymn — a cosmic panorama from the creation of the angels to the final fire and the renewal of all things

works.title: Ascetical Homilies (First Part)
works.authorDate: Isaac of Nineveh (Isaac the Syrian) · c. 660–700 CE (7th century)
works.form: Collection of discourses (homilies/treatises) · East-Syrian (Church of the East) mystical-ascetical theology
works.tagline: God is not one who requites evil but who sets evil right — the most radical theology of divine mercy in the Christian tradition

works.title: Kitab al-Hayawan (Book of Animals) (Early)
works.authorDate: Al-Jahiz (Abu Uthman Amr ibn Bahr) · c. 860
works.form: Encyclopaedic treatise · Abbasid-era Mutazili natural philosophy and adab literature
works.tagline: Seven volumes of zoological observation, Qur'anic exegesis, and proto-evolutionary thinking — the natural world as proof of divine wisdom

works.title: Tarikh al-Rusul wa al-Muluk (History of Prophets and Kings) (Early)
works.authorDate: Al-Tabari (Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Jarir) · c. 915
works.form: Universal chronicle · Islamic historiography based on isnad (chains of transmission)
works.tagline: The most comprehensive universal history of the early Islamic world — from creation through the prophets to the Abbasid caliphate, transmitted through chains of scholarly authority

works.title: Kitab al-Tawasin (Book of the Ta-Sin) (Early)
works.form: Mystical treatise (short prose meditations with verse) · Sufi mystical theology
works.tagline: Mystical meditations on Muhammad's light, Moses's encounter with the burning bush, and Iblis's tragic refusal — the cost of absolute love

works.title: Sayings and Prayers (reconstructed from Attar and others) (Early)
works.authorDate: Rabia al-Adawiyya (of Basra) · c. 8th century (preserved in later sources, esp. Attar, 13th century)
works.form: Sayings, prayers, and anecdotes (hagiographic compilation) · Sufi devotional tradition (mahabba)
works.tagline: The foundational utterances of Sufi love-mysticism — love God for God alone, not for paradise or against hell

works.title: Pramanavarttika (Commentary on Valid Cognition) (Early)
works.form: Philosophical treatise (verse with auto-commentary) · Buddhist epistemology (pramana); Yogacara-Sautrantika synthesis
works.tagline: The most rigorous Buddhist treatise on logic and epistemology — perception and inference as the only valid means of knowledge, deployed against Brahmanical authority and the permanent self

works.title: Slokavarttika (Early)
works.form: Verse commentary (varttika) · Mimamsa (Bhatta sub-school)
works.tagline: The most powerful philosophical defence of the Vedas — authorless scripture, intrinsic validity, and the defeat of Buddhist momentariness

works.title: Vakyapadiya (On Words and Sentences) (Early)
works.form: Philosophical treatise (verse with prose commentary) · Vyakarana (Sanskrit grammatical philosophy)
works.tagline: Language is Brahman — the sphota as eternal meaning-bearer, the sentence as primary unit, and grammar as the path to liberation

works.title: Platform Sutra of the Sixth Patriarch (Liuzu Tanjing) (Early)
